stories.title: Greece derails EU’s Russia sanctions plan
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — EU countries failed to reach a deal on sweeping new sanctions against Russia on Wednesday night, after Greece blocked the measures to protect its fuel shipping industry. Wednesday’s talks, which wrapped after 7.30 pm, were just the latest in a series of efforts to secure the unanimous agreement needed on the slew of energy and trade measures designed to drain Moscow’s war chest. The package was presented in June, but discussions over the details have dragged on, despite significant pa

stories.summary: Brussels talks between senior diplomats now underway in attempt to sign off 21st package of sanctions, after previous failed attempts The Venice Biennale said it would challenge a European Union decision to withdraw a €2 ⁠million grant in response to the return of Russia to its 2026 edition, despite continuing aggression on Ukraine, Reuters reported. stories.title: Trump threatens retaliatory strikes on key Iranian infrastructure as conflict escalates
stories.summary: President Donald Trump on Wednesday warned that the United States would strike Iranian bridges and power plants if Iran continues to attack commercial ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz. In  a post on Truth Social , Trump proclaimed that any type of Iranian attack on vessels traveling through the waterway would trigger corresponding American strikes on key infrastructure, including locations in or around Tehran. “From this point forward, any time the Islamic Repu

stories.title: Zelenskyy ousts Ukraine army chief after protests - The Latest
stories.summary: Volodymyr Zelenskyy has fired his commander-in-chief, Oleksandr Syrskyi, in Ukraine’s biggest military shake-up in two years. Nationwide protests broke out after Zelenskyy sacked Mykhailo Fedorov as defence minister, who the Ukraine president says will now be brought back into government in a ‘prominent position’. Lucy Hough speaks to the Guardian’s senior international correspondent Luke Harding – watch on YouTube Continue reading...

stories.title: Zelenskyy under growing pressure to reinstate sacked defence minister
stories.summary: Campaigners in Ukraine pledge to continue nationwide protests until Mykhailo Fedorov gets his job back Volodymyr Zelenskyy was under growing pressure to reinstate his sacked defence minister on Wednesday, after campaigners said they would continue indefinite nationwide protests until Mykhailo Fedorov got his job back. stories.title: Mykhailo Drapatyi: Ukrainians hope new commander will end army’s ‘Soviet culture’
stories.summary: Drapatyi has long been a critic of the military’s command culture, but experts warn change could take time Through a haze of smoke, a lone Ukrainian armoured vehicle suddenly burst into view. The BMP-2 accelerated towards a barricade of tyres and debris, smashing through it in seconds as protesters scattered. The European Commission said Paramount’s offer to ⁠end a film ⁠distribution ​joint venture with Universal Pictures addressed its concerns over the potential merger. stories.title: Opposition parties shut down Indian parliament in solidarity with ‘cockroach’ protests
stories.summary: Proceedings suspended as politicians call for Modi government to address police violence and education scandals India’s opposition parties have shut down parliament in a protest calling for the resignation of the education minister, in solidarity with the new youth-led “cockroach” movement . stories.title: Poll: Trump’s MAGA base is souring on his Iran war
stories.summary: Half of President Donald Trump’s MAGA base thought Iran’s war was worth its economic costs in May. Now, just a little more than a third do. That precipitous, 13-point drop in just two months, according to  a new POLITICO Poll , reveals how the president’s most die-hard supporters are starting to agree with the majority of Americans who have long opposed the U.S.’ involvement in the war. Additionally, 37 percent of self-identified MAGA Trump voters say the U.S. should continue its invol

Speaking to journalists at the Farnborough International Airshow in one of his first engagements in the job, Streeting said that as a “friend and ally,” Israel should be held to the same standard Britain holds itself to. “That is the standard that I think, I fear, they’ve fallen short of,” he added. stories.title: France records more than 5,700 excess deaths during June heatwave
stories.summary: Figures come as large parts of southern Europe endure another record-breaking period of extreme temperatures France recorded more than 5,700 excess deaths during its June heatwave, authorities have said, as swathes of southern Europe continue to endure another record-breaking period of extreme temperatures. The French national health agency said on Wednesday that it had recorded 5,764 excess deaths ⁠between ​17 June and 2 July, an excess mortality rate of 36%. Continue reading...

stories.summary: New prime minister plans to make Sarwar – the first senior Labour figure to call for Keir Starmer’s resignation – a peer Scottish Labour’s rivals in Holyrood have slammed Anas Sarwar’s shock decision to resign as leader to sit in Andy Burnham’s new government, calling him a “chancer” and accusing him of lying to voters. stories.title: Charles Gaines, whose book Pumping Iron helped to popularize bodybuilding, dies at 84
stories.summary: 1974 book led to making of seminal documentary and introduced Arnold Schwarzenegger to the world Charles Gaines, the author whose book and documentary Pumping Iron helped bring professional bodybuilding into the mainstream and introduce Arnold Schwarzenegger to the world, has died. He was 84. Gaines died on Tuesday, according to Greta Gaines, the author’s daughter. She did not reveal a cause. Continue reading...

stories.title: Tories ask HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on £5m gift
stories.summary: Party chair queries status of funds Reform leader received from crypto billionaire Christopher Harborne The Conservatives have asked HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage should have paid tax on the £5m gift he received from the crypto billionaire Christopher Harborne. The party chair, Kevin Hollinrake, wrote to the tax authorities questioning whether it was a payment to compensate him for running to be an MP. Continue reading...

stories.title: Woman, 70, charged with murder of British expat Karen Carter in France
stories.summary: Public prosecutor for the region said suspect had been initially arrested shortly after the murder on 29 April last year A 70-year-old woman has been charged with the murder of Karen Carter, the British expat found dead in south-western France last year. The woman was charged on 2 July with Carter’s murder in Trémolat, in the Dordogne, and is “under judicial supervision”. Continue reading...

stories.summary: Prosecutors seek a one-year sentence and heavy fine after chef garnished dish with insects not approved for consumption Prosecutors in South Korea have asked a court to sentence the owner of a Michelin-starred restaurant to one year in prison for serving a dessert topped with ants, an insect not approved for consumption under local food safety law. stories.summary: Isabel Rose, 26, sentenced to six years for blackmail and perverting course of public justice over her allegations against British man A British woman who reported being raped to police in Hong Kong has been sentenced to six years in prison for blackmail and perverting the course of public justice. Isabel Rose, 26, from south-east London, travelled to Hong Kong in early 2024 to visit a British banker she had met while travelling in south-east Asia. Continue reading...

stories.title: EU says Trump’s new generic drug tariffs won’t hit its exports
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — The European Commission said Wednesday that it expects European generic medicines to be exempt from U.S. President Donald Trump’s latest tariffs, arguing that Washington committed to a zero tariff rate with last year’s transatlantic trade deal. Trump on Tuesday announced a plan to impose a 100 percent tariff on generic drugs starting in August 2028 as part of a bid to bring pharmaceutical production back to the United States. stories.summary: The new PM’s priorities are domestic, but he will soon realise that closer engagement with the EU is a no-brainer What will European leaders make of Andy Burnham, the seventh UK prime minister they have had to deal with in a decade of chronic political instability in London unleashed by the fateful 2016 referendum vote to leave the European Union? stories.title: Sperm whales change ‘vowel’ sounds when boats are nearby, scientists discover
stories.summary: Researchers listening off the Caribbean island of Dominica found variations in the mammals’ audible clicks when marine traffic was close Sperm whales produce a specific pattern of clicking noises when they hear the noise of boats, researchers have found. The pattern is so distinct that the scientists who identified it said they can now use it to predict when ships are nearby. stories.title: Health worker monitored for Ebola in London hospital after working in DRC
stories.summary: UK resident was evacuated from Democratic Republic of the Congo where there is an outbreak of the disease A humanitarian worker is being monitored at a specialist hospital in London after potential exposure to the Ebola virus. stories.title: Spanish hotel chain ceases operations in Cuba under pressure from Washington
stories.summary: Meliá group says Donald Trump’s sanctions make it ‘impossible’ to keep any of its 34 sites open A Spanish hotel chain is to cease all operations in Cuba at the end of the week due to “major difficulties” in doing business on the island under growing pressure from Washington. The latest US sanctions make it “impossible, de facto and de jure, to maintain even minimal operational stability”, the Meliá group explained in a statement on Tuesday to the Spanish stock market regulator. The agreement, which was signed in Brus

stories.title: France and UK to increase staffing at border controls in effort to avert travel chaos
stories.summary: Disruption at Channel crossings expected to rise amid new fingerprint and facial recognition checks France and the UK have agreed to increase staffing at border controls in response to warnings of travel chaos caused by new fingerprinting and facial recognition checks. Disruption at Channel crossings is expected to rise sharply next weekend at the start of the summer holiday season, with MPs saying there would be “utter chaos and miles of tailbacks” unless the EU’s entry-exit system (EES) is fix

stories.title: Donate now to a Top 10 CNN Hero
stories.summary: Anderson Cooper explains how you can easily donate to any of the 2021 Top 10 CNN Heroes.

stories.title: Sign up to our newsletter for a weekly roundup of travel news
Big-box stores could help slash emissions and save millions by putting solar panels on roofs. Why aren't more of them doing it?
stories.summary: As the US attempts to wean itself off its heavy reliance on fossil fuels and shift to cleaner energy sources, many experts are eyeing a promising solution: your neighborhood big-box stores and shopping malls.

stories.title: Not only is Lake Powell's water level plummeting because of drought, its total capacity is shrinking, too
stories.summary: Lake Powell, the second-largest human-made reservoir in the US, has lost nearly 7% of its potential storage capacity since 1963, when Glen Canyon Dam was built, a new report shows.

stories.title: These were the best and worst places for air quality in 2021, new report shows
stories.summary: Air pollution spiked to unhealthy levels around the world in 2021, according to a new report.

stories.title: CNN Heroes: Sharing the Spotlight
The surreal facades of America's strip clubs
stories.summary: Some people travel the world in search of adventure, while others seek out natural wonders, cultural landmarks or culinary experiences. But French photographer François Prost was looking for something altogether different during his recent road trip across America: strip clubs.

stories.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
stories.summary: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

stories.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
stories.summary: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

stories.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
stories.summary: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

stories.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
stories.summary: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

stories.title: How Playboy cut ties with Hugh Hefner to create a post-MeToo brand
stories.summary: Hugh Hefner launched Playboy Magazine 70 years ago this year. The first issue included a nude photograph of Marilyn Monroe, which he had purchased and published without her knowledge or consent.

stories.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
stories.summary: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

stories.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
stories.summary: Paul Templer was living his best life.

stories.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color
stories.summary: When customers at Silicon Valley Bank rushed to withdraw billions of dollars last month, venture capitalist Arlan Hamilton stepped in to help some of the founders of color who panicked about losing access to payroll funds.

stories.title: Analysis: Fox News is about to enter the true No Spin Zone
stories.summary: This is it.

stories.title: Retail spending fell in March as consumers pull back
stories.summary: Spending at US retailers fell in March as consumers pulled back amid recessionary fears fueled by the banking crisis.

stories.title: Markets digest bank earnings after recent turmoil
Still haven't filed your taxes? Here's what you need to know
stories.summary: So far this tax season, the IRS has received more than 90 million income tax returns for 2022.

stories.title: Look of the Week: Blackpink headline Coachella in Korean hanboks
stories.summary: Bringing the second day of this year's Coachella to a close, K-Pop girl group Blackpink made history Saturday night when they became the first Asian act to ever headline the festival. To a crowd of, reportedly, over 125,000 people, Jennie, Jisoo, Lisa and Rosé used the ground-breaking moment to pay homage to Korean heritage by arriving onstage in hanboks: a traditional type of dress.

stories.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
stories.summary: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.
